parameters.content:
## 1. Start with realistic forecast changes

The manual trigger feeds three sample requests into the workflow. Edit `Load forecast requests` to use your real account, owner, workbook cell, proposed value, and approval thresholds.

Keep at least one approved and one review example while testing so both branches stay visible.

## 2. Normalize rows before calling Bilig

`Validate request rows` rejects non-numeric values and unsupported workbook cells before any HTTP request runs.

Use this node for your CRM, form, or queue validation rules. Keep validation close to the input rows so bad data fails early.

## 3. Recalculate the workbook and verify readback

`Build Bilig request` sends only `sheetName`, `address`, and `value`. `Call Bilig WorkPaper` edits the workbook, recalculates formulas, exports/restores JSON, and returns proof.

For private forecasts, replace `baseUrl` in step 1 with your own Bilig service URL.

## 4. Block stale results before routing approvals

`Verify formula proof` stops the workflow unless Bilig returns fresh formula output, persisted formulas, restored JSON readback, and a changed computed value.

`Apply approval policy` turns that verified workbook output into an `approve` or `review` decision.

## 5. Replace the demo output with your destination

The IF node creates separate approved and review records so downstream systems receive clear payloads.

Replace `Write audit summary` with Slack, Gmail, HubSpot, Airtable, or your queue once the sample output is right. Keep the verification fields in the final payload.
